other_observers: Kara Lewantowicz; I was the original finder. I described why the bird was a Glossy Ibis to Kara, helping me remember all of the plumage details to look for.
specific_location: about 1 mile east on Hanover Road from its intersection with the Old Pueblo Road.
description: I observed both of the Colorado Plegadis Ibis in a flooded field. Here (an see photo) were the differences in the two species that I observed.  Glossy Ibis  Head with dark brown eye. Bill light brownish.   LIGHT BLUISH LINES run from bill base up to sides of eye by do not encirle the eye.   Legs grayish with red "knees"Behaviors: Foraging in a flooded field with about 14 White-face IbisCall: no vocalization heardPlumage: alternate
